name: Le Mont Fourcat – trail starting from the Brougues car park
name_original: Le Mont Fourcat – trail au départ du parking des Brougues
region_intro: Jogging around Mercus-Garrabet offers a challenging experience through a mountainous landscape. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, with routes often leading to peaks like Mont Fourcat and Roc du Taus. Runners can expect trails that traverse varied terrain, including steep ascents and descents, providing panoramic views of the surrounding area. The natural environment here is well-suited for those seeking demanding running routes. Best jogging routes around Mercus-Garrabet The most popular jogging route is Le Mont Fourcat – trail starting from the Brougues car park, a 7.0 miles (11 km) trail that takes 2 hours to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain, leading towards Mont Fourcat. Another top favourite among local runners is Mont Fourcat Shelter – Mount Fourcat 2,002 m loop from Saint-Paul-de-Jarrat, a difficult 16.8 miles (27 km) path. This challenging loop includes a significant climb to Mount Fourcat, offering extensive mountain views. Local runners also love the Mont Fourcat Shelter – Mount Fourcat loop from Mercus-Garrabet, a 16.0 miles (26 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with high elevation, often completed in about 4 hours 49 minutes. Jogging around Mercus-Garrabet is defined by challenging mountain trails, significant elevation gains, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for various ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult running routes. The routes in Mercus-Garrabet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 3500 runners have used komoot to explore Mercus-Garrabet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Mercus-Garrabet?
A: There are over 200 running routes in the Mercus-Garrabet area. These include a variety of options, with 12 easy, 71 moderate, and 136 difficult trails, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Mercus-Garrabet?
A: Jogging in Mercus-Garrabet primarily involves challenging mountain trails with significant elevation changes. You can expect varied terrain, including steep ascents and descents, often leading to panoramic viewpoints of the surrounding Ariège Mountains.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Mercus-Garrabet?
A: Yes, while the region is known for its challenging mountain trails, there are 12 easy running routes available in Mercus-Garrabet. These provide a gentler introduction to the area's natural beauty.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Mercus-Garrabet?
A: The running routes in Mercus-Garrabet are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Runners often praise the challenging mountain terrain and the extensive panoramic views from peaks like Mont Fourcat.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Mercus-Garrabet?
A: Yes, Mercus-Garrabet offers several circular running routes. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6983574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Fourcat Shelter – Mount Fourcat 2,002 m loop from Saint-Paul-de-Jarrat&lt;/a&gt; is a 16.8-mile (27 km) path offering extensive mountain views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24683837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roc du Taus loop from Montoulieu&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 15.2 km route.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while running in Mercus-Garrabet?
A: Many running routes in Mercus-Garrabet offer stunning views and pass by significant landmarks. You can encounter mountain peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1576306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Fourcat&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/101523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Barthélemy Peak&lt;/a&gt;. There are also viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5935356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ariège Mountains from Clot de la Carbonnière&lt;/a&gt;, providing expansive vistas.

Q: Are there any running trails that lead to mountain passes?
A: Yes, the mountainous landscape of Mercus-Garrabet includes several passes that can be incorporated into running routes. You might encounter passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1098976" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Lauze Pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/889136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas de Souloumbrie&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5842971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porte-Pa Pass&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique challenges and views.

Q: What is a challenging long-distance running option in the area?
A: For a challenging long-distance run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20538053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Fourcat 2,002 m – Mont Fourcat Shelter loop from Mercus-Garrabet&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult route spans approximately 32 kilometers and features significant elevation gain, making it a demanding option for experienced runners.

Q: What is the typical duration for a difficult running route in Mercus-Garrabet?
A: The duration for difficult running routes in Mercus-Garrabet varies significantly based on distance and elevation. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974172120/le-mont-fourcat-trail-starting-from-the-brougues-car-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le Mont Fourcat – trail starting from the Brougues car park&lt;/a&gt;, a 7-mile (11 km) difficult trail, typically takes around 2 hours to complete. Longer routes, like the 16-mile (26 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24684699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Fourcat Shelter – Mount Fourcat loop from Mercus-Garrabet&lt;/a&gt;, can take nearly 5 hours.

Q: Are there any specific peaks that running routes often lead to?
A: Many running routes in Mercus-Garrabet are centered around prominent peaks. Mont Fourcat is a frequent destination, with several trails leading towards or around it, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974172120/le-mont-fourcat-trail-starting-from-the-brougues-car-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le Mont Fourcat – trail starting from the Brougues car park&lt;/a&gt;. Roc du Taus is another significant peak featured in routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24683837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roc du Taus loop from Montoulieu&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Old stone shelter located near the Cabane de la Lauzate (Cabane du Mt Fourcat), itself closed due to vandalism.
